detaileddescription.para: Enable the data cache.
Disable the data cache.
Flush the whole data cache.
Invalidate the whole data cache.
Flush and Invalidate the whole data cache.

briefdescription.para: Flush an address range in the d-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Flush the specified address range of the data cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being flushed, all the portions of the data structures sharing the same line will be flushed. This is usually not a problem because writing back is a non-destructive process that could be triggered by hardware at any time, so having an aligned addr or a padded size is not strictly necessary.

briefdescription.para: Invalidate an address range in the d-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Invalidate the specified address range of the data cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being invalidated, all the portions of the non-read-only data structures sharing the same line will be invalidated as well. This is a destructive process that could lead to data loss and/or corruption. When addr is not aligned to the cache line and/or size is not a multiple of the cache line size the behaviour is undefined.

briefdescription.para: Flush and Invalidate an address range in the d-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Flush and Invalidate the specified address range of the data cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being flushed, all the portions of the data structures sharing the same line will be flushed before being invalidated. This is usually not a problem because writing back is a non-destructive process that could be triggered by hardware at any time, so having an aligned addr or a padded size is not strictly necessary.

parameterdescription.para: Starting address to flush and invalidate.

detaileddescription.para: The API is provided to dynamically detect the data cache line size at run time.
The function must be implemented only when CONFIG_DCACHE_LINE_SIZE_DETECT is defined.

parameterdescription.para: If the d-cache is not enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Enable the instruction cache.
Disable the instruction cache.
Flush the whole instruction cache.
Invalidate the whole instruction cache.
Flush and Invalidate the whole instruction cache.

briefdescription.para: Flush an address range in the i-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Flush the specified address range of the instruction cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being flushed, all the portions of the data structures sharing the same line will be flushed. This is usually not a problem because writing back is a non-destructive process that could be triggered by hardware at any time, so having an aligned addr or a padded size is not strictly necessary.

briefdescription.para: Invalidate an address range in the i-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Invalidate the specified address range of the instruction cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being invalidated, all the portions of the non-read-only data structures sharing the same line will be invalidated as well. This is a destructive process that could lead to data loss and/or corruption. When addr is not aligned to the cache line and/or size is not a multiple of the cache line size the behaviour is undefined.

briefdescription.para: Flush and Invalidate an address range in the i-cache.

detaileddescription.para: Flush and Invalidate the specified address range of the instruction cache.

simplesect.para: the cache operations act on cache line. When multiple data structures share the same cache line being flushed, all the portions of the data structures sharing the same line will be flushed before being invalidated. This is usually not a problem because writing back is a non-destructive process that could be triggered by hardware at any time, so having an aligned addr or a padded size is not strictly necessary.

parameterdescription.para: Starting address to flush and invalidate.

detaileddescription.para: The API is provided to dynamically detect the instruction cache line size at run time.
The function must be implemented only when CONFIG_ICACHE_LINE_SIZE_DETECT is defined.

parameterdescription.para: If the d-cache is not enabled.
